source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure that Code Signing is enabled for Lambda functions.

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure that Code Signing is enabled for Lambda functions.
input_row.description: Ensure that all your Amazon Lambda functions are configured to use the Code Signing feature in order to restrict the deployment of unverified code.
input_row.rationale: Code Signing, ensures that the function code is signed by an approved (trusted) source, and that it has not been altered since signing, and that the code signature has not expired or been revoked.
input_row.audit: From the Console 1. Login to the AWS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/lambda/ 2. In the left column, under AWS Lambda, click Functions. 3. Under Function name click on the name of the function that you want to review. 4. Click the Configuration tab 5. Under General configuration on the left column, click Code signing. 6. Under the Code signing configuration section check for any code signing configurations created for the function. 7. If there are no code signing configurations available or listed is not enabled, refer to the remediation 8. Repeat steps 2-7 for each Lambda function within the current region. 9. Then repeat the Audit process for all other regions. From the Command Line 1. Run aws lambda list-functions aws lambda list-functions --output table --query "Functions[*].FunctionName" This command will provide a table titled ListFunctions 2. Run aws lambda get-function-code-signing-config aws lambda get-function-code-signing-config --function-name "name_of_function" --query "CodeSigningConfigArn" 3. The command output should return an array with the requested ARN(s) 4. If the get-function-code-signing-config command output returns null, there are no code signing configurations for the Lambda function. 5. Refer to the remediation below. 6. Repeat step 2-5 for each Lambda function available in the selected AWS region. 7. Perform the Audit process for all other regions used.
input_row.remediation: From the Console 1. Login to the AWS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/signer 2. Click on Create Signing Profile if none are set up. If you already have some created in the left panel click on Signing Profiles, Create Signing Profile. ***Note a Signing Profile is a trusted publisher and is analogous to the use of a digital signing certificate to generate signatures for your application code. 3. On the Create Signing Profile setup page provide Profile name Specify the Signature Validity period (6 months up to 12 months is recomended) 4. Click on Create Profile 5. Go to the Amazon Lambda console https://console.aws.amazon.com/lambda/. 6. In the left panel, under Additional resources, click on Code signing configurations. 7. Click on Create configuration 8. On the Create code signing configuration setup page: • Description box - provide a short description to identify this configuration • Click inside the Signing profile version ARN box and select the Signing Profile created above. • For Signature validation policy, click the signature validation policy suitable for your Lambda function. **Note - A signature check can fail if the code is not signed by an allowed Signing Profile, or if the signature has expired or has been revoked. • Click Enforce – blocking the deployment of the code and also issue a warning. • Click Create configuration  9. Go to the Amazon Lambda console https://console.aws.amazon.com/lambda/. 10. Click Functions. 11. Under Function name click on the name of the function that you want to review 12. Click the Configuration tab 13. In the left menu click Code signing. 14. Click Edit 15. On the Edit code signing, select the code signing configuration created above from the drop down 16. Click Save The Lambda function is now configured to use code signing. 17. Next Upload a signed .zip file or provide an S3 URL of a signed .zip made by a signing job in AWS Signer. 18. To start a signing job, go to AWS Signer console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/signer. 19. In the left panel, click on Signing Jobs. 20. Start a Signing Job to generate a signature for your code package and place the signed code package in the specified destination path. 21. Start Signing Job setup page: - Select the Signing Profile created in dropdown list. - Code asset source location, specify the Amazon S3 location of the code package (.zip file) to be signed. Only S3 buckets available in the current region are displayed and can be used - Signature destination path with prefix where the signed code package should be uploaded. - Start Job to deploy your new Signing Job - Job status reads Succeeded, you can find the signed .zip package in your assigned S3 bucket. 22. Publish the signed code package to the selected Lambda function. 23. Amazon Lambda will perform signature checks to verify that the code has not been altered since signing **Note - The service verifies if the code is signed by one of the allowed signing profiles available. 24. Repeat steps for each Lambda function that was captured in the Audit.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- This control can be verified using AWS APIs and CLI commands, specifically for checking Lambda function configurations related to code signing.
- The AWS CLI and boto3 provide methods to retrieve information about the code signing configurations of Lambda functions.
- The audit field provides specific AWS CLI commands which indicate the capability to automate this check using available AWS APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_lambda_function_code_signing_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API calls or boto3 SDK methods to use:** 
  - Use `boto3` Lambda client method `list_functions()` to list all Lambda functions.
  - For each function, use `get_function_code_signing_config()` to retrieve the Code Signing Configuration.
- **AWS CLI commands (if applicable):**
  - `aws lambda list-functions --query "Functions[*].FunctionName"` to list all functions.
  - For each function, `aws lambda get-function-code-signing-config --function-name <function_name> --query "CodeSigningConfigArn"` to check if there's an associated Code Signing Configuration.
- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Lambda functions across all regions in the account.
- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Ensure the `CodeSigningConfigArn` is not `null`, indicating a code signing configuration is present.
- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** Each Lambda function must have a non-null `CodeSigningConfigArn`.
  - **Fail:** If `CodeSigningConfigArn` returns `null`, the Lambda function is not configured with a code signing configuration. 

This automated audit approach will systematically verify the presence of code signing configurations for Lambda functions across AWS accounts and regions, ensuring compliance with the CIS benchmark control.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. This control can indeed be verified using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI commands. The methods and commands mentioned are appropriate for checking Lambda function configurations related to code signing.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The use of `boto3` and AWS CLI commands is valid for retrieving and checking the code signing configuration of Lambda functions.
- The boto3 method `get_function_code_signing_config()` and the CLI command `aws lambda get-function-code-signing-config` are correctly identified for checking the presence of a code signing configuration.
- The program name `aws_lambda_function_code_signing_enabled` is descriptive but could be more specific to indicate that it checks for the presence of a code signing configuration.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `aws_lambda_function_code_signing_config_present` to more accurately reflect the check being performed.
- Consideration should be given to checking across all regions and handling potential exceptions if a function does not have a code signing configuration.
- It might be beneficial to include a check for the validity of the code signing configuration itself, not just its presence.
- The audit should ensure that the `CodeSigningConfigArn` is not only present but also correctly configured according to organizational policies.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded, and the use of AWS APIs and CLI commands is appropriate for the automated approach. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach is feasible.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `aws_lambda_function_code_signing_config_present` for clarity.
- Ensure the audit script includes error handling for functions without a code signing configuration and checks across all AWS regions.
- Consider adding a validation step for the configuration itself to ensure it meets organizational security requirements.
